meta.name: OASIS Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale
meta.fullName: Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale
meta.description: The OASIS is a brief 5-item scale that assesses the frequency, intensity, and functional impairment of overall anxiety, applicable across all anxiety disorder types.
meta.author: Norman et al. (2006)
meta.reference: Norman SB, et al. Development and validation of an Overall Anxiety Severity and Impairment Scale (OASIS). Depression and Anxiety. 2006;23(4):245-249.
meta.instruction: Please answer about your anxiety over the past week.

questions.text: In the past week, how often have you felt anxious
When you have felt anxious, how intense or severe was your anxiety
How often did you avoid situations, places, or activities because of anxiety
How much did anxiety interfere with your ability to do work, school, or household tasks
How much did anxiety interfere with your social life and relationships

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your anxiety level is within the normal range, with no significant anxiety distress detected. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current mental health
Keep up regular exercise and relaxation habits
Maintain a strong social support network
Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching

ranges.label: Mild Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests mild anxiety. You may occasionally feel tense or worried, but the impact on daily functioning is limited. Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
suggestions: Learn and practice deep breathing and progressive muscle relaxation
Maintain regular physical exercise
Identify and address your anxiety triggers
Consider consulting a professional if anxiety continues to increase
Learn and practice progressive muscle relaxation

ranges.label: Moderate Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests moderate anxiety. Anxiety may be noticeably interfering with your work, social life, or daily activities. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider seeking help from a counselor or psychiatrist
Systematically learn anxiety management techniques such as cognitive-behavioral strategies
Avoid excessive use of caffeine and alcohol
Establish regular routines and dedicated relaxation time
Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist

ranges.label: Severe Anxiety
ranges.description: Your score suggests severe anxiety, which may be significantly impairing your work ability, social functioning, and quality of life. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional psychological or psychiatric treatment as soon as possible
Inform family or friends about your condition for support
Avoid avoidance behaviors; try to gradually face anxiety-provoking situations in a safe environment
If in crisis, please call a mental health crisis hotline
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
